# af pr invokes the configured [pr].cmd proxy with token substitution for base,
# head, and title. The [pr.flag_template] entries append typed flags so callers
# can drive --body and --draft through the proxy argv.

env HOME=$WORK/home
mkdir $HOME/.config/af
mkdir $HOME/.local/share/af/v1/sessions/demo
cp config.toml $HOME/.config/af/config.toml
cp state.toml $HOME/.local/share/af/v1/sessions/demo/state.toml

# Base command tokens are substituted and the body flag_template is appended.
exec af pr demo --title T --body B
stdout 'PR base=main head=feat/demo title=T'
stdout '--body B'

# --draft triggers the draft flag_template entry.
exec af pr demo --title T --draft
stdout 'PR base=main head=feat/demo title=T'
stdout '--draft'

-- state.toml --
schema_version = 1

[session]
name = "demo"
status = "active"
created_at = 2026-05-22T10:00:00Z

[worktree]
path = "/tmp"
branch = "feat/demo"
base_branch = "main"

-- config.toml --
schema_version = 1

[pr]
cmd = ["echo", "PR", "base={base}", "head={head}", "title={title}"]

[pr.flag_template]
body = ["--body", "{body}"]
draft = ["--draft"]
